key_insight: OrchRM learns a reward model for multi-agent orchestration from self-supervised win-lose pairs built from intermediate execution artifacts, avoiding expensive human labels and sub-agent rollouts.
engineering_relevance: It offers a scalable way to train and scale LLM-based orchestrators more efficiently, reducing token use by up to 10x while improving test-time accuracy by up to 8%.
method: Use intermediate artifacts from multi-agent runs to create preference pairs.
Train a Bradley-Terry reward model at the orchestration level.
Use the learned reward to guide orchestrator training and test-time scaling.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports consistent gains across mathematical reasoning, web QA, and multi-hop reasoning, with up to 10x lower token usage and up to 8% higher accuracy.
watch_item: Performance depends on the quality of intermediate artifacts and preference construction, so noisy orchestration traces could weaken reward learning.
